# Army UAS Marketplace — Buyer Account & Access Guide

> **Last updated:** April 2026  
> **Audience:** Military units, DoD agencies, government partners, and allied nation personnel attempting to access the marketplace to browse and procure UAS systems

---

## The Short Answer

The Army UAS Marketplace does **not** have open public self-registration. Access is controlled. If you're a .mil user, you need your CAC and a network path to the portal. If you're a non-.mil government partner or allied nation, access requires coordination through your chain of command or a designated Army liaison.

If someone or something told you there's a simple sign-up flow — that's either outdated, incorrect, or referring to a different system. The marketplace is two weeks old as of this writing and the access process is still being refined.

---

## Who Can Access the Marketplace

The Army has stated the marketplace is available to three categories of users:

**1. Army units and DoD personnel**  
Active duty, Reserve, and Guard units with a procurement need. Access is expected to be CAC-authenticated through Army Enterprise systems. Your S4/G4 or aviation officer is likely your unit's access point.

**2. Other U.S. government agencies and partners**  
Federal agencies with a valid requirement can access through interagency agreements. Contact ACC-Redstone (see contacts below) to establish access.

**3. Allied nations / Foreign Military Sales**  
The marketplace is being positioned to support FMS, but this pathway is still maturing. Allied partner access flows through the standard FMS/Security Cooperation process — your country's security cooperation officer is the right starting point.

---

## How to Get Access as a .mil User

Public documentation on the exact login URL and account provisioning is not yet widely published. Here's the confirmed path:

**Step 1 — Contact your S4/G4/Aviation officer**  
The marketplace was showcased at the AUSA Global Force Symposium (March 24–26) and AAAA Warfighting Summit (April 14). Units that attended those events were introduced to the portal directly. Your battalion/brigade S4 may already have access or be in the process of getting provisioned.

**Step 2 — Go through PMO UAS**  
The Project Management Office for UAS (PM UAS) at Redstone Arsenal manages the marketplace. For access questions, reach out to the POCs listed in CSO `W58RGZ-26-S-C001` on SAM.gov:

- **Miguel Harris** — miguel.c.harris.civ@army.mil  
- **Latoya Pryor** — latoya.s.pryor.civ@army.mil

Even if you're a buyer and not a vendor, these are your best current contacts to get directed to the right access request process.

**Step 3 — Attend the Marketplace Showcase (April 14)**  
The Army is hosting a Marketplace Showcase on **April 14, 2026**, prior to the AAAA Warfighting Summit. This is the single best event for units and buyers to get hands-on access, see a live demo, and get provisioned. Check SAM.gov for registration details.

---

## What the Marketplace Does Once You're In

Once a user has access, the platform (built on AWS with the Army Enterprise Cloud Management Agency) provides:

- **Browse and compare** — side-by-side comparison of vetted UAS systems by spec, Group classification, and capability
- **Soldier feedback** — direct feedback mechanism from the field back to PM UAS and vendors
- **Order placement** — submit procurement requests directly through the portal

Orders are **not** immediately fulfilled at the click of a button. Per Army officials, all transactions go through established contracting procedures before completion — the marketplace accelerates the process, it doesn't bypass statutory requirements.

---

## The C-UAS Marketplace Is Different

There are two separate Army drone-related marketplaces. Don't confuse them:

| Marketplace | Manager | What It Is |
|---|---|---|
| **UAS Marketplace** | PM UAS / ACC-Redstone | Buy drones (Group 1–3 UAS platforms) |
| **Counter-UAS Marketplace** | JIATF 401 / ACC-Aberdeen | Buy anti-drone systems and components (1,600+ items) |

If you need **counter-drone tech** (sensors, effectors, C2 systems), that's the JIATF 401 / Common Hardware Systems portal, not this one. That marketplace uses the CHS (Common Hardware Systems) website with a separate access process managed through Army Contracting Command – Aberdeen Proving Ground (ACC-APG).

---

## If You're a Non-DoD Government Agency (PS-LE, DFR, etc.)

Public safety, law enforcement, and first responder agencies are not the primary target audience for this marketplace — it's focused on DoD, other federal agencies, and allied nations. For PS-LE drone procurement:

- **Blue UAS List** platforms are your primary vetted procurement path (see [Blue UAS Compliance Guide](/compliance/))
- **DFR/public safety grants** — FEMA BSIR, BJA, and state homeland security grants can fund Blue UAS compliant platforms
- **CISA** has separate guidance for critical infrastructure operators

If your agency has an existing DoD partnership or interagency agreement, contact ACC-Redstone about whether your organization qualifies for marketplace access.

---

## Status as of April 2026

The marketplace launched March 24, 2026 — it's brand new. Expect:

- Access provisioning processes to be informal and relationship-driven right now
- Documentation to improve significantly over the next 60–90 days
- New C4S amendments to CSO W58RGZ-26-S-C001 posted on SAM.gov as the catalog expands
- Group 3 UAS expected to be added by summer 2026 (subject to FY27 funding)
- Blue UAS List systems and Gauntlet competition winners to be added on a rolling basis

The current 30-system catalog is entirely drawn from existing PMO UAS programs of record. The real expansion — and the point where the marketplace becomes a meaningful procurement tool for most units — happens as those additional pipelines are integrated.

---

## Quick Reference

| Need | Action |
|---|---|
| .mil unit access | Contact your S4/G4 or PM UAS POCs directly |
| See a live demo | Attend the April 14 Marketplace Showcase |
| Vendor account (sell on marketplace) | See the [Vendor Submission Guide](/vendor/dow-uas-marketplace) |
| Counter-drone tech (not UAS platforms) | CHS website via JIATF 401 / ACC-APG |
| Allied nation / FMS access | Security Cooperation Officer / FMS case officer |
| General access questions | miguel.c.harris.civ@army.mil |

---

> **Note:** The Army has not published a public-facing URL for the marketplace portal. If you find one, the April 14 showcase or your chain of command are the most reliable paths to confirmed access. This guide will be updated as the access process matures.
